description: Evolution is the fundamental biological process by which heritable characteristics of biological populations change over successive generations. This process, driven by mechanisms such as mutation, natural selection, genetic drift, and gene flow, leads to the diversity of life on Earth, the adaptation of organisms to their environments, and the emergence of new species over vast spans of time.

Property.name: heritable-trait-change
Property.description: The fundamental process involves the alteration of heritable characteristics within populations across generations.

Property.name: mechanism-driven-process
Property.description: Evolution is propelled by specific biological mechanisms including natural selection, mutation, genetic drift, and gene flow.

Property.name: biodiversity-adaptation-generation
Property.description: The long-term outcome of evolution is the generation of diverse life forms and their adaptation to various environments.

Property.name: long-temporal-process
Property.description: Evolution typically unfolds over vast spans of time, often requiring many generations for significant changes to accumulate.

Property.name: population-level-change
Property.description: Evolutionary changes occur at the level of populations, not individual organisms, affecting their genetic makeup over time.

Property.name: genetic-variation-dependency
Property.description: Genetic variation within a population is a prerequisite for evolutionary change, providing the raw material for selection.

Property.name: environmental-interaction
Property.description: Organisms interact with their environment, which exerts selective pressures influencing the direction of evolutionary change.

Property.name: historical-record-formation
Property.description: Evolution leaves a historical record in the form of fossils and genetic lineages, allowing for its study and reconstruction.
